title: The Briefing
tagline: AI intelligence for Gauntlet AI — April 3, 2026

items.headline: Claude Code Source Leak Exposes 512,000 Lines and Unreleased Models
items.summary: Anthropic accidentally shipped a source map with Claude Code v2.1.88 on March 30, letting intern Chaofan Shou decode and publish 512,000 lines across 1,900 files. The code was forked 40,000+ times before Anthropic pulled it. Engineers found references to an unreleased Claude 4.6 variant codenamed 'Capybara' and model 'Numbat,' plus a hidden 'undercover mode' that lets the agent commit to public repos without leaving a signature.

items.headline: OpenAI Kills Sora — Web Access Ends April 26, API Closes September 24
items.summary: OpenAI is shutting down Sora after it burned roughly $1M/day and peaked at 1M daily active users before falling by half. Sora 2 Pro ranks 19th on Artificial Analysis's text-to-video leaderboard, behind ByteDance, Kling, xAI, and Google. The Sora team will redirect to world models and robotics; OpenAI will also consolidate its browser, Codex, and ChatGPT app into a single desktop application.

items.headline: Inside Claude Code: 40+ Tool Modules, Swarm Subagents, Three-Tier Memory
items.summary: Analysis of the leaked source reveals Claude Code is built like a small OS: 40+ tools with individual permission gates, subagent swarms with a controller agent, and a three-tier memory system (MEMORY.MD index → Markdown files → JSON transcripts). Upcoming features hiding behind 'false' flags include 'Kairos' — an always-on background agent with an 'autoDream' logic system that prunes and consolidates memory — plus a voice interface and a subagent called 'Ultraplan' for cloud-offloaded heavy tasks.

items.headline: TTT-E2E: LLMs That Learn at Inference Keep Latency Flat to 128K Tokens
items.summary: Researchers at Astera Institute, Nvidia, Stanford, UC Berkeley, and UC San Diego introduced Test-Time Training End-to-End (TTT-E2E), a 3-billion-parameter transformer that compresses context into its weights during inference rather than re-attending to the full sequence. On an H100 GPU, latency grew by only 25ms per 1,000 tokens (vs. 12–70ms for a standard transformer) across 8,000–128,000 tokens. Accuracy slightly exceeded a vanilla transformer on most benchmarks, though it struggles on Needle-in-a-Haystack tasks beyond 8K tokens.

items.headline: Google Drops Lyria 3 to 750M+ Gemini Users — Trained on Licensed Music, SynthID Watermarked
items.summary: Google's Lyria 3 is a latent diffusion model that generates 30-second audio clips (MP3/MP4) with instruments, vocals, and lyrics in eight languages from text or image inputs. It's live now on Gemini (free for 18+) and YouTube Shorts via Dream Track. Unlike Suno and Udio — which settled with UMG after copyright suits — Lyria 3's training data is fully licensed, outputs are SynthID-watermarked, and Google filters outputs against existing music to avoid sonic likeness matches. Days after launch, Google acquired ProducerAI (formerly Riffusion) to deepen its professional production lineup.

items.headline: OpenAI–Disney Partnership Effectively Dead as Sora Shuts Down
items.summary: In late 2025, OpenAI announced a high-profile deal with Disney: OpenAI would license Disney characters, train on Disney footage, and Disney would invest up to $1 billion. Disney planned to show Sora-generated content on Disney+ and use it for pre-production and marketing. With Sora's shutdown confirmed, the partnership is effectively over, and OpenAI's Sora team pivots to longer-horizon world model and robotics research.

items.headline: AI Dev 26 Agenda Released — Google DeepMind, Oracle, AMD Speakers Confirmed
items.summary: DeepLearning.AI's AI Dev 26 conference agenda is out, featuring two days of talks, workshops, and demos hosted by Andrew Ng, with teams from Google DeepMind, Oracle, and AMD presenting. The event is a must-watch for AI engineers and practitioners looking to map out the latest in applied AI development.

items.headline: Voice UIs Are Closer Than You Think — Vocal Bridge Offers Free Developer Tools
items.summary: Andrew Ng's editorial this week argues voice UIs will complement (not replace) keyboards and mice, just as touch gestures unlocked a new class of mobile apps. Vocal Bridge, an AI Fund portfolio company led by CEO Ashwyn Sharma, built a dual-agent architecture that keeps foreground conversation latency low while a background agent handles complex agentic workflows. Developers added voice to existing apps in under an hour at a recent DeepLearning.AI + AI Fund hackathon.
